top_prompts: {animal} ke liye roz ka paani, shed aur chara routine simple batao. mudda clean water and trough cleaning hai.

top_first_safe_steps: First, keep trough water clean and shaded.
Make the routine practical: clean water, dry floor, airflow, safe fodder, and rest around clean water and trough cleaning.
For clean water and trough cleaning, write down time, feed, water, dung, milk, breathing, and standing changes for later trained help.
First, offer water breaks and shade during hot work.
Make the routine practical: clean water, dry floor, airflow, safe fodder, and rest around summer water breaks.
For summer water breaks, write down time, feed, water, dung, milk, breathing, and standing changes for later trained help.
First, remove wet bedding and improve drainage.
Make the routine practical: clean water, dry floor, airflow, safe fodder, and rest around dry shed floor.
For dry shed floor, write down time, feed, water, dung, milk, breathing, and standing changes for later trained help.
First, keep airflow without chilling young calves.
top_first_checks: Check whether clean water and trough cleaning is already causing appetite, dung, or standing changes, and note whether the animal is standing and breathing normally.
For clean water and trough cleaning, ask who else in the herd is affected and whether feed, work, milking, or weather changed recently.
For clean water and trough cleaning, ask when it started, what the farmer already tried, and whether trained help is reachable later.
Check whether summer water breaks is already causing appetite, dung, or standing changes, and note whether the animal is standing and breathing normally.

Check whether monsoon ventilation is already causing appetite, dung, or standing changes, and note whether the animal is standing and breathing normally.
top_red_flags: For clean water and trough cleaning, escalate if you see not drinking, collapse, breathing trouble, or many animals sick.
For clean water and trough cleaning, escalate if you see collapse, severe weakness, breathing trouble, or many animals affected.

For summer water breaks, escalate if you see not drinking, collapse, breathing trouble, or many animals sick.

For dry shed floor, escalate if you see worsening signs, blood, deep wound, abnormal milk, or unsafe movement/sale pressure.
